mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models without requiring additional dependencies like Istio or Knative. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation for optimal performance and the potential complexity of managing model versions.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeAI Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for KubeAI.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeAI operator in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage AI models.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> This command installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for deploying an AI model, which KubeAI will manage.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  modelType: llm
  modelName: gpt-3
EOF

```

> This command creates a sample model resource for KubeAI.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  modelType: llm
  modelName: gpt-3
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai

```

> This command lists the models in the kubeai namespace and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all models managed by KubeAI, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> This command uninstalls the KubeAI Helm release.
> This command deletes the kubeai namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important model configurations. This step involves upgrading KubeAI to the latest version and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-model-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get models -n kubeai

```

> Backup existing model configurations.
> This command upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the models are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > kubeai-model-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create model: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the model.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits for the model or scale up the cluster nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model name in the Custom Resource does not match any available models.

**Fix:**
Check the model name in the Custom Resource and ensure it matches an existing model.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeAI operator is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI operator pod may have crashed or failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the KubeAI operator pod with 'kubectl logs -l app=kubeai -n kubeai' and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions for KubeAI have not been installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using the command: 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get models -n kubeai' showing your models with a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
  namespace: kubeai
spec:
  modelType: llm
  modelName: gpt-3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
